review_title: Form and Sheets in One Platform for Manage Almost All of Business Thing
author: Rio M.
pros: Retable has a clean UI, secure database, and almost all the features I need.
cons: The storage is not too large for a business.

description: My overall experience with retable is very good, it's very good Airtable alternative, but since it is new in the market, it will take some time to catch up with Airtable, but Its sure that this is the best Airtable alternative available in the market right now.

pros: Editing data in the view using the shared link for the table, without logging into the account.
cons: Missing advance Internal automation featureSharing views missing

author: Connie H.
pros: I liked that it was online and can be a collaborative tool.
cons: People did not know about it and did not use it.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: One stop solution for your business problems
author: Suresh S.
pros: Retable is a great tool for increasing productivity and teamwork in teams. Real-time collaboration with documents, spreadsheets, and presentations is made easier by its many useful features. Integration of APIs for automation is also excellent. Keyboard shortcuts are incredibly helpful and come in handy when writing and testing test cases. Customer service is very proactive. I urge everyone to try out this application.
cons: While using the tool, there were a few times we experienced downtime, and I believe the user interface could be improved as well. Other than that its a great application.
author_profession: Director IT Infrastructure and Security Information
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Feedom to own your own data
description: I am use it since about 4 months ago. We are still using it partially to back up some key data. And analyze the marketing campaigns. We are considering to use it also for project management.
author: Raul F.
pros: The production process involves a lot of differents apps, so it is good not to depend on single app but to be able to export your data to a secure warehouse. For example, we use several Email Service Providers for our marketing campaigns, different tools to create surveys, several order payment gateways, etc. So our data is in different locations. And it is good to be able to change suppliers knowing that our customer's or product's information is well store in an specific place. And we can update and analyze it.
cons: The app is quite new and with fast development. I think we are not taking all the advantage of it that we could.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Turbocharge your spreadsheets into Smart Data
description: It is an ideal software that will revolutionize the way you work with data and spreadsheets.
author: Kapil R.
pros: Retable lets you do a lot more with your data by letting you manage it seamlessly. It is refreshingly different from other options in the market.
cons: Unlimited enterprise plans should be cheaper.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Perfect for tracking the jobs to do
description: Creating records for the client requests
author: Tayfun A.
pros: The produsct is easy to use and have too many features to use.
cons: Exporting the tables could be more compatible but its fine.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: good but not great airtable alternative
author: Jan G.
pros: Low price (with lifetime deal) and almost all features for a professional use.
cons: The UI is not so polished as Airtables. Things are always a bit less intuitive or "nice".
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Retable has earned a seat at the table
author: David T.
pros: Retable has been great because the database function is very straightforward to use and out the gate was able to be used to run an API. In addition the speed of development has been nothing short of excellent. It is a relatively new tool and it has gone from useful to excellent really fast.
cons: The only real complaint is that databases are still databases. I dont have any specific Retable cons, just that the more technical you are the more you can get out of it, but you can get a ton of value even if you are not technical. I am moderately technical and have gotten a lot out of it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Smart Database management platform that is in continuous growth!
author: Ahmed Farrag Gamal A.
pros: I like mostly the use of use, and more importantly the big amount of templates that can be used for may industries (Marketing, Sales, Project Management and Remote Work). The idea behind the product is simple but very important: it is a collaborative spreadsheet but also Retable has Online forms that I find useful in many occasion.I also like the stability of the product. Haven't faced any noticeable issues since I joined it.The customer support is great!
cons: Honestly the product is not perfect but personally, for my use case, I don't notice any downside. It works fine for me, as it should be!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Un Nuevo competidor para tomar en cuenta
author: Orlando A.
pros: - Precio/funcionalidades: Precios de los planes muy competitivos, incluso ante otros con semejantes características.- Multiplataforma: Se puede acceder desde el navegador, la aplicación de escritorio y recientemente Android (necesitan mejoras) y tengo entendido pronto saldrá app para iOS.- Agradable sistema colaborativo.- Es fácil iniciar un nuevo proyecto desde cero o plantillas existentes
cons: - Rendimiento: Tiene cierta lentitud al momento de cargar los datos, a veces parece que se atasca, por lo que en ocasiones podría ser molesto.- Documentación: La documentación es algo limitada, algo normal en software en desarrollo. Sin embargo, están mejorando en este aspecto.- UX/UI: Hay ciertos aspectos de la experiencia del usuario y estéticos que podrían mejorarse.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The interface is really easy, so you don't have any adaptation problems and you can easily integrate your existing data to Retable. Therefore, you save time in the operational process. Creating various views for the data you want on different bases and managing all from a single platform makes things easier and increases efficiency. You can also easily see that it is improving day by day.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I was using excel for my work almost every day, but its interface was starting to make things really boring. I discovered Retable while searching for alternatives. Retable's UI is really easy-to-adapt and I also can use the templates in order to make a new structure. The templates makes the process easier. Now, creating spreadsheet is easier and more comfortable for me.
cons: It fully meets my needs, there no cons to me.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

cons: iOS app should be there, which is currently not there, also if there could be the Card view available,it would be beneficial.

review_title: Retable is a powerful data management tool
description: Retable is addressing the issue of more effectively managing and organizing structured data. It aids me in creating tools that are easier to use for non-technical users.
author: santosh M.
pros: Retable offers fantastic collaboration tools. Team projects are ideal for spreadsheets because multiple users can edit the same spreadsheet at once. Real-time updates guarantee that everyone is using the most recent information.
cons: Retable is a small business with a small customer service staff. As a result, it might take longer for users to receive assistance when they do.

review_title: Elevate Your Data Management with Retable
author: Curtis O.
pros: The forms to table functionality streamlines data collection and organization. It eliminates manual data entry, saving time, and reducing potential errors, thereby enhancing efficiency and accuracy in managing data.
cons: If I have to pick something as a negative, the UI/UX is maybe my least favorite. It's not bad, and It's probably just a personal thing, but I don't love the feel of the app.
